data.title: 'No Force on Earth Could Stop the Fallout,' Anthropic CEO Warns Trillion-Dollar AI Spending
data.description:
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warns that the AI boom may be moving too fast in spending. In a new podcast interview, he said some rivals are taking big risks on data center build-out without fully grasping the math behind it.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I get the impression that some of the other companies have not written down the spreadsheet, that they don't really understand the risks they're taking," Amodei said on the Dwarkesh Podcast. He added that some firms are "just doing stuff because it sounds cool."

His remarks come as AI leaders plan to invest heavily in chips, power, and new sites to train and run models.

Infrastructure Bets Are Getting Bigger

At the center of the debate is OpenAI. Last year, OpenAI outlined plans that could total about $1.4 trillion to build up to 30 gigawatts of compute power over time. Chief Executive Sam Altman has also spoken about adding one gigawatt a week at full scale.

Amodei did not name OpenAI, but he described a risk that fits such plans. "If you're buying a trillion dollars a year of compute for 2027 because you think you're going to make a trillion dollars in revenue, and instead you make $800 billion, no force on earth could stop the fallout," he said.

In simple terms, he is pointing to a mismatch risk. Data centers take one to two years to build. Power deals must be locked in early. If revenue falls short even by 20%, the fixed costs remain. That gap can strain cash flow and debt.

This matters for public firms tied to the AI buildout. Nvidia Corporation (NVDA) sells the chips at the core of these data centers. Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Amazon (AMZN) are all pouring billions into AI cloud gear. If demand stays strong, those bets may pay off. However, if growth slows, the market could reassess the actual capacity needed.

Enterprise Focus Versus Consumer Scale

At the same time, Anthropic is not standing still. Reports suggest it aims to secure at least 10 gigawatts of compute over the next few years, which could cost hundreds of billions of dollars.

Still, Amodei framed his firm as more measured. "We've thought carefully about it. We're an enterprise business. Therefore, we can rely more on revenue. It's less fickle than consumer demand," he said.

In addition, Anthropic said it will pay for 100% of grid upgrades tied to its sites and cover any rise in power costs for local users. That move may ease pushback and speed up projects, which have become more complex as power demand rises.

For investors, the broader issue is not whether AI works. Most leaders in the field, including Amodei, still see rapid progress ahead. The real question is timing. Can revenue scale fast enough to match the scale of new build plans?

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable publicly traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Claude. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: AI Video Arms Race Expands as ByteDance Enters the Field, Raising Stakes for NVDA and GOOGL
data.description:
ByteDance, the parent company of TikTok, has launched Seedance 2.0, a new AI model that generates a short video from a single text prompt, with a clear story, voice, and sound. The clips run up to 15 seconds and will soon be available to global users through CapCut, which has 642 million monthly active users.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The release places ByteDance in direct competition with OpenAI and Alphabet (GOOGL), which offer their own text-to-video tools. However, its distribution seems to be the bigger story.

The TikTok Playbook Moves to AI Video

Seedance 2.0 can create scene changes, voice-overs, and character motion from a simple prompt. A film director who tested the tool said, "Storyboarding has long been considered the one area where human thinking and innovation really shone. It's now been proven that AI can replace it."

That statement highlights how fast AI video tools are improving. At the same time, Seedance will be built into CapCut, which already sits at the center of short-form video creation. This gives ByteDance a built-in global base, rather than relying on a new platform.

In contrast, OpenAI's Sora is still limited to select users. Google's Veo is tied to YouTube and cloud tools. ByteDance is placing AI video inside an app that creators already use daily. As a result, the adoption curve could move faster.

Compute Demand and Legal Risk

There is also a hardware angle. Video generation requires heavy computing power. ByteDance is nearing a deal to access more than 7,000 Nvidia (NVDA), Blackwell B200 chips in Indonesia, according to people familiar with the matter. Those chips, made by Nvidia, cannot be shipped directly to China due to U.S. export controls. Still, the move shows how AI video could add to long-term demand for advanced chips.

Meanwhile, legal concerns are building. The Motion Picture Association said the model used U.S. copyrighted works without approval "on a massive scale." In addition, a filmmaker claimed the tool produced audio that sounded nearly identical to his voice, despite not using a voice sample. ByteDance later suspended a feature that allowed the creation of digital avatars based on real people.

In a broader perspective, AI video could disrupt content production and editing software. However, copyright claims and privacy risk may slow global rollout, especially in the U.S.

Overall, Seedance 2.0 signals that AI video is becoming a core battleground. ByteDance may not lead in chat tools, but in short-form video creation, it is building on a base that already spans hundreds of millions of users.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to assemble notable AI firms that employ an AI video generator. This is a useful tool to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: UBER Expands Delivery to Drive $1 Billion in Growth, Says It's Time to 'Raise the Bar'
data.description:
Uber Technologies (UBER) plans to launch its delivery service in seven new European countries this year. The move was first reported by the Financial Times. The company will enter the Czech Republic, Greece, and Romania. In addition, it will roll out in Austria, Denmark, Finland, and Norway. Uber expects this push to add about $1 billion in gross bookings over the next three years.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Susan Anderson, Uber's global head of delivery, told the Financial Times it was time to "raise the bar, shake things up and deliver better value across the category." That comment signals that Uber sees room to gain share in markets where food delivery is still growing.

Meanwhile, UBER shares dropped 1.73% on Friday, closing at $69.99.

data.title: Are the Markets Open Tomorrow, Washington's Birthday?
data.description:
The U.S. stock markets will be closed tomorrow, February 16, 2026, in honor of Washington's Birthday.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Keep track of all stock market holidays with TipRanks' Stock Market Holidays calendar.

Will the Holiday Effect Impact the Markets This Week?

Understanding how the stock market might be affected by the holiday will help you invest well and beat the markets. Investors sometimes refer to the pre-holiday market rise and the post-holiday price drift as the "holiday effect."

The holiday effect is linked to investors' general optimism and confidence. The fact that customers tend to spend more money around the holidays is one explanation for the pre-holiday market surge. The share prices of retailers, in particular, may increase as a result.

As for the post-holiday price drift, one reason for it is that many traders and market analysts take time off work during the holidays to relax from their regular trading and investment research schedules. Those investors view the holidays as a time to unwind and refresh. Alternatively, some traders see the season as a chance to work harder and get some tactical advantages in the market, maybe by taking advantage of the period when many other traders take time off.

Read TipRanks News to learn about Washington's Birthday holiday effect on the stock market. And remember to always use TipRanks to stay up-to-date on your stock research.

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Feb. 15
data.description:
U.S. stocks ended the week lower as investors moved away from big tech and focused more on safer areas of the market. The S&P 500 (SPX) fell 1.28%, the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) dropped 1.27%, and the Dow (DJIA) slid 1.15%. At the same time, the 10-year Treasury yield eased to about 4.09%, while gold (CM:XAUUSD) jumped above $5,000 and oil (CM:CL) held near $63 a barrel.

data.title: 2 'Perfect 10' Stocks Analysts Say Deserve a Closer Look Now
data.description:
One day down, and one day up – the markets are showing a steady churn of volatility that has investors on edge. They're not necessarily pulling out, but they are clearly uncertain about the future. And that kind of uncertainty tends to feed on itself.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The macro picture isn't easing the confusion. Economic signals have been mixed, geopolitical tensions remain elevated, and while AI adoption continues to expand at a rapid pace, the massive spending required to support it is raising concerns. Heavy capital outlays, stretched valuations, and the recent bout of software-related panic have reminded investors that even transformative trends can create short-term turbulence.

In this kind of environment, investors naturally look for stocks that can deliver solid returns regardless of shifting market conditions. That's easier said than done. Successful investing often comes down to constructing the right portfolio balance – blending growth potential with resilience and selectivity.

One way to bring structure to that process is through data-driven tools. TipRanks' Smart Score is designed to aggregate and interpret a wide range of market signals, from analyst ratings and insider activity to hedge fund positioning and technical indicators. It processes large volumes of real-time trading data and distills it into a straightforward rating system on a scale of 1 to 10. Stocks that earn a "Perfect 10" score stand out based on a combination of quantitative and qualitative factors, suggesting they merit further examination. When those high scores are reinforced by supportive views from Wall Street analysts, the signal can become more compelling.

With that in mind, we turned to the TipRanks database to identify "Perfect 10" stocks that also carry recent analyst backing. Below are two names that stand out in the current landscape and warrant a closer look.

Ralph Lauren (RL)

We'll start in the world of fashion with Ralph Lauren, our first 'Perfect 10' stock and one of the most enduring names in global luxury apparel. Founded in 1967 by its namesake designer, Ralph Lauren built the company around an aspirational vision of American style, blending heritage, sport, and refinement into a brand identity that has remained remarkably consistent for decades while continuing to evolve with changing consumer tastes.

Today, the company operates as a diversified lifestyle platform rather than simply an apparel label. Its portfolio is led by Polo Ralph Lauren but also includes Purple Label, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, and a range of licensed categories such as fragrances and eyewear. In addition to apparel for men, women, and children, the brand extends into accessories and home collections, reinforcing its positioning as a complete lifestyle ecosystem. Maintaining tight control over branding, distribution, and design has been central to protecting that premium image, sustaining pricing power, and supporting long-term brand equity.

Ralph Lauren products are sold in more than 50 countries through a mix of wholesale partners, company-owned stores, outlet locations, and a steadily expanding direct-to-consumer and digital channel. In recent years, management has emphasized brand elevation by reducing promotional activity and prioritizing full-price selling, a strategy that has helped strengthen margins while reinforcing the company's upscale positioning in a competitive global retail landscape.

That approach was evident in the company's fiscal third quarter of 2026 results, where revenue reached $2.4 billion, representing a 12% year-over-year increase and coming in ~$96 million ahead of consensus expectations. Growth was broad-based, but Asia stood out as the primary driver, with regional revenue climbing 22% compared to the prior year, while China alone delivered a 30% increase on sustained brand demand and strong digital engagement.

The bottom line reflected similar momentum, as non-GAAP EPS came in at $6.22, up 29% year over year and 41 cents above forecasts, supported by higher average unit retail prices, disciplined inventory management, and continued strength in full-price sales. Taken together, the results reinforce the view that Ralph Lauren's premium positioning, disciplined execution, and global brand resonance are translating into both revenue growth and margin expansion, even amid a mixed consumer backdrop.

For Jefferies analyst Blake Anderson, Ralph Lauren's overall profile – its quality products and strong growth – are the key points. He says of the company: "Ralph Lauren stands out among fashion brands given its heritage of quality and timeless style, diversified portfolio and distribution, and balanced growth algo. We expect +MSD% rev growth driven by a combination of AUR, share gains in emerging categories, and international white space (namely APAC) and margin expansion led by SG&A leverage, cost controls, and fewer promos leading to +LDD% EPS growth supported by buybacks. We view premium P/E valuation as appropriate given growth, healthy balance sheet, solid ROIC, and strong mgmt execution."

To this end, Anderson rates RL shares a Buy, and gives the stock a $410 price target that suggests the stock has an 11% upside in the offing for the year ahead. (To watch Anderson's track record, click here)

Overall, the 14 recent analyst reviews on record for Ralph Lauren's stock include 12 Buys and 2 Holds, supporting the Strong Buy consensus rating on the shares. RL is selling for $369.18 and its $418.15 average target price indicates a one-year upside potential of 13%. (See RL stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Why Is Gold Up Today, 2/13/26?
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) is up by over 2% on Friday and has reclaimed the $5,000 level after a cooler-than-expected inflation print led to rising rate cut odds. Gold tends to benefit when rates are lower, as the opportunity cost of holding the precious metal relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, decreases.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On an annual basis, the Consumer Price Index (CPI) fell to 2.4% in January from 2.7% in December, marking the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. January's core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was 2.5%, the lowest level since March 2021.

Gold Rebounds as Rate Cut Hopes Grow

While a rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 is unlikely, the odds of a 25 bps reduction at the April 29 meeting climbed to 26.4% from 23.9% a day ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool. The odds are even higher at the June 17 meeting, sitting at 52.3% from 48.9% yesterday.

Gold's move today "suggests the correction may have overshot, with bargain‑hunting and position‑adjustments now providing support," said ING Bank commodity strategist Ewa Manthey. The precious metal has returned 16.4% year-to-date and has completely recovered its losses over the past week.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Film Pros Plan "Tooth and Nail" Fight against Warner / Netflix Deal, Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips
data.description:
Entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) may be facing a proxy fight over the deal to be bought by Netflix (NFLX), but it may have an even bigger fight on its hands from the film industry itself. Stuart Ford, a major name in the independent film circuit, has some dire pronouncements and deep concerns about the state of the film industry should this deal go through. Warner shareholders seemed concerned as well, and shares slipped fractionally in the closing minutes of Friday's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stuart Ford is chairman of AGC Studios, and he made it clear that a Netflix/Warner deal would be a disaster. Sufficient disaster, in fact, to require the strongest possible response. Basically, a deal like that would turn entertainment professionals into the equivalent of an "Uber (UBER) driver" that would cost the entire industry talent. Ford called on professionals to "…fight tooth and nail to preserve the business culture of money flowing through the system…."

Ford further noted, "We're only just now starting to feel the effects in the industry of the flow of money having become interrupted, if not completely cut off, over the last 10 years as a result of streaming becoming such a major part of the distribution pipeline." The biggest objection seems to flow from Netflix's tendency to keep residuals and other profit participation types out of the hands of filmmakers, which would effectively reduce them to per-piece talent. If sufficient quantities of talent rebel, meanwhile, it could mean a big win for other streaming providers, and a huge potential loss for Netflix, which depends on fresh content to keep viewers.

Political Wrestlers?

Meanwhile, a recent event during All Elite Wrestling (AEW) saw Brody King removed from the schedule for a while. Some questioned whether or not it was related to anti-Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) chants before one of his matches. King has been clear in his opposition to ICE, but following the fans' chants of support, King did not appear on the following episode of AEW Dynamite, reports noted.

However, this was not deliberate, Warner noted. Warner clarified in a statement, saying, "Warner Bros. Discovery did not have any involvement in Brody King's upcoming AEW schedule. Any speculation to the contrary is categorically false. Brody is scheduled to appear during the next AEW event, which will air this Saturday on TNT and HBO Max."

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on four Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 171.86% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $28.86 per share implies 3.22% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
